Skillloom card

Browser Ritual Receipt

Turn a browser QA pass into a polished launch card.

Skillloom packages an agent skill or workflow brief into a README hero and a glossy one-page landing card, so tiny tools can look launch-ready before they need a full website.

Web agents, generative UI, and skill packaging are all rising at once. awesome-web-agentsLightAgentOpenGenerativeUIopenpencil

Audience

  • Indie builders shipping microsites, workflow packs, agent skills, and tiny CLIs.

Artifacts

  • README hero block you can paste into a repo instantly
  • Editorial HTML card with product framing and tasteful layout
  • Compact summary JSON for other automation steps

Why it exists

Many agent skills and workflow packs are useful but visually anonymous. Skillloom gives them a front door with almost no extra work.

What you ship

A single manifest becomes a polished HTML page, a copy-ready README hero, and a summary file that can plug into larger publishing workflows.

Why this week

Recent GitHub signal shows fast-moving activity around web agents, AI-native design tools, generative UI, and skill packaging. This idea sits where all four overlap.

Commands

npm testnpm run buildopen dist/index.html
